title: Pega Platform '25 — Mobile Solutions
content:
# Pega Platform '25 — Mobile Solutions

## Overview
Pega Platform '25 provides a comprehensive low-code environment for building model-based mobile solutions that exceed the scope and functionality of browser-based enterprise applications. Whether you are a professional or citizen developer, you can develop robust, secure, and customized mobile apps with modern user interfaces using the mobile Channel, and then distribute these apps to your users.

Every Pega application can become a mobile app. With top-class case management, Pega Mobile puts an emphasis on handling and getting work done anywhere, anytime. You can deploy a Pega application as a stand-alone mobile app, embed it into existing apps or websites, or access it from a mobile browser.

## UI Architecture Options

Pega Platform '25 offers two UI architectures for mobile apps and one legacy design system:

### Constellation (Recommended)
- The latest Pega UI offering low-code configuration and efficient development
- Requires less effort to use and maintain; templates and prefabricated components reduce time to value
- Provides a prescriptive experience with native Android and iOS components for the run-time interface
- After Pega Platform 8.8: Cosmos React → Constellation design system, DX API v2 → Constellation DX API, SDKs → Constellation SDKs
- Supports: Case creation, Case Actions, Assignments, Worklist, Pulse comments, push notifications
- Constellation apps in '25 do NOT support: full offline mode, signature control, barcode scanning, custom modules, selected top-level pages (Documents/Spaces/Notifications), URL fields as images
- Read-only offline: Users can view offline-enabled Cases/Assignments, edit Case details, view attachments (images, PDFs)

### Traditional UI (Theme Cosmos)
- Section-based architecture with ready-to-use templates, patterns, and themes
- Supports a wider variety of mobile features including full offline mode
- Interface relies on web components
- Ideal when you need more than what Constellation currently offers for offline

### UI Kit (Legacy — Do Not Use for New Apps)
- Legacy design system no longer actively developed
- Consists of sample end-user UI in the form of design resources (rules, skins)
- Migrate existing UI Kit apps to Constellation or Traditional UI for better performance

## Mobile Features Overview

### Low-Code Authoring Features
- **Out-of-the-box mobile Channels**: Framework to immediately start mobile app development
- **Prescriptive authoring**: Guided app development process; Constellation design system provides features out-of-the-box
- **Advanced search**: Global search with run-time access to Case records; Constellation includes it by default, Traditional UI requires search gadget setup
- **Offline mode configuration**: Constellation supports read-only offline (view Cases, Assignments from Worklist); Traditional UI adds ability to edit data, advance Cases, upload attachments
- **Branding and styling**: Customize visual elements; Constellation offers designed/tested Views optimized with proven UX; Traditional UI requires more design/configuration
- **One-click build**: Generate native iOS and Android apps in a simplified process; preview before building

### Native Experience Features
- **Secure authentication**: Pega Platform credentials to corporate identity provider with OpenID Connect (OIDC)
- **Responsive UI components**: Mobile-ready elements with native transitions for adaptive, modern experience
- **OS and device dependency handling**: Pega Platform automatically manages differences between operating systems and devices
- **Push notifications**: Increase engagement and collaboration through automatic notifications
- **Device capabilities**: Camera, geolocation tracking, barcode scanning integration
- **Custom modules**: Extend functionality with custom native code; bring elements into external customer-facing apps

### Offline Scenarios
- **Field workers support**: Maintain business continuity with specific offline tasks
- **Data collection**: Traditional UI users can collect data via questionnaire forms
- **Data always available**: Offline data catalogs (addresses, parts) for field workers
- **Attachments available offline**: Traditional UI — attachments (videos, PDFs) seamlessly available offline, users can add new attachments; Constellation UI — read-only viewing/downloading of attachments (images, Word docs, PDFs)

## Mobile OS and Browser Support

### Pega Mobile Client
- Mobile Build Server always uses the latest Pega Mobile Client release
- All applications are backwards-compatible — no need to use latest Pega Platform to run latest Mobile Client
- Huawei mobile devices no longer supported (due to US regulations)

### Supported Operating Systems
- **iOS/iPadOS**: iOS or iPadOS 17 and 18
- **Android**: Android 9.0 and later; must target Android 15 (API level 35) for Google Play Store; use Pega Mobile Client 13.0.0+
- **Browsers**: Apple Safari, Google Chrome, Microsoft Edge Chromium (Android, PMC 13.2.1+)

## Constellation UI for Mobile Apps

### Designing the User Interface
- Configure Visibility conditions to display or hide UI elements based on device type (mobile vs desktop)
- Use the Layout tab in the mobile Channel to configure layout and add content
- Constellation offers prescriptive design with pre-optimized Views

### Supported Constellation UI Offline Features
- Display offline data and provide ability to edit Case details while offline
- Users can view offline-enabled Assignments, view attachments on forms
- After reconnecting, data synchronizes automatically with the server
- Supported offline fields include text, numeric, date, dropdown, checkbox, radio button, and more
- Offline Data Page support: Read-only data pages available offline for lookups

### Synchronization Indicators
- **Green checkmark**: All data is synchronized and up to date
- **Blue arrow**: Changes available, synchronization in progress
- **Yellow warning**: Synchronization pending (offline)
- **Red dot**: Synchronization error occurred

### Adding Offline Capabilities to Existing Constellation Apps
- Can add offline mobile capabilities at any time as long as the application was configured to support offline when created
- Two deployment options: (1) Read-only offline with Constellation, (2) Full offline using Constellation with web portals + offline-enabled Traditional UI app sharing the same parent framework

## Developing Mobile Apps

### Setting Up Mobile Apps
1. Create a mobile Channel to configure, customize, and generate your mobile app
2. With Constellation UI, you can use the default mobile Channel or create a new one
3. Traditional UI might require a new mobile Channel to avoid inheritance issues with Case Types

### Development Workflow
1. Setting up mobile apps (create mobile Channel)
2. Adding content (configure list pages, case pages, menu bar)
3. Selecting Case Types for offline-enabled apps
4. Previewing mobile apps (use Pega Mobile Preview app)
5. Enhancing mobile apps (branding, push notifications, localization)

### Authoring List Pages
- Configure the list page layout, sorting, filtering, and search functionality
- List pages show summaries of Cases to users for quick navigation

### Authoring Case Pages
- Populate Case Pages with fields, insights, and widgets
- Give Cases and data objects visual representation
- Use supported Actions for mobile apps (Constellation supports specific action set)
- Consolidate Case Pages into the Case Designer for unified management

### Adding Items to the Menu Bar
- Menu bar displays icons and labels for up to 5 items
- Configure items based on channel type (mobile, desktop, mobile browser)
- Use Configure icon to set up tab Views

### Creating Offline Configuration Templates
- Enable support for components in offline mode
- Exclude Data Pages from packaging in offline-enabled mobile apps
- Exclude Data Pages from delta synchronization
- Make custom resources available in offline mode
- Route Case Status conflicts in offline mode

## Adaptive and Responsive Mobile Interfaces

### Adaptive Interfaces
- Change how UI components display in a View depending on the user's device
- Hide complex elements (like charts) on mobile screens to save space
- Configure adaptive behavior using device-specific conditions

### Responsive Interfaces
- Automatically adjust layout based on screen size using breakpoints
- Content reflows and resizes without hiding elements
- Configure breakpoints for different media types (phone, tablet, desktop)

## Enhancing Mobile Apps

### Applying Custom Themes
- Customize the icon, logo, and launch screen of your mobile app
- Adjust color theme of UI elements (header, background)
- Constellation: Customize theme through design system configuration
- Traditional UI: Use branding assets templates for customization

### Branding Assets Templates
- Download branding assets template for your Pega Mobile Client release (e.g., branding-assets-13.1.zip for PMC 12.7.0+)
- Package must have expected structure or build process shows warning

## Push Notifications

### Overview
- Improve user engagement with automatic push notifications
- Notifications can be triggered by case events, assignments, SLA deadlines, and custom business logic
- Supported on both iOS and Android

### Configuring Push Notifications for Android
- Uses Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM) for delivery
- Configure the FCM server key in Pega Platform
- Set up notification categories and templates

### Configuring Push Notifications for iOS
- Uses Apple Push Notification Service (APNs)
- Requires APNs certificate or key configuration
- Configure in the mobile Channel settings

### Push Console
- Send push notifications directly from the Pega Platform push console
- Target specific users, roles, or all mobile app users
- Track notification delivery history

## Generating Native Mobile Apps

### Build Process
- When development is complete, generate an installation package for testing and distribution
- Pega uses a cloud-based Mobile Build Server to compile native apps
- One-click build process generates iOS (.ipa) and Android (.apk/.aab) packages

### Mobile Build Server Settings
- Configure server connection settings in the mobile Channel
- Set Mobile Client version (default: Official — always latest)
- Configure build options for debug or release builds

### Default Security Settings
- Apps are generated with secure default settings
- HTTPS required for server communication
- Certificate pinning available for enhanced security

### Preparing Apps for Signing
**Android**: 
- Obtain a signing certificate (keystore file)
- Create an Android certificate set in Pega Platform
- Google Play requires apps to be signed for distribution

**iOS**:
- Obtain an App ID from Apple Developer portal
- Get a signing certificate and provisioning profile
- Create an iOS certificate set in Pega Platform
- Options for development, ad-hoc, and App Store distribution profiles

### Building Mobile Apps
- Trigger the build from the mobile Channel page
- Monitor build progress in the build status section
- Download completed builds as installation packages

### Generating for Different Environments
- Build separate packages for development, staging, and production environments
- Configure server URLs and settings per environment

## Securing Mobile Apps

### Security Mechanisms
- **Authentication**: Multiple methods from Pega credentials to corporate identity provider via OIDC
- **Session management**: Configurable session timeouts for security
- **Data encryption**: All data stored on device is encrypted
- **Certificate pinning**: Validate server certificates to prevent man-in-the-middle attacks
- **Biometric authentication**: Fingerprint and face recognition for app unlock

### Selecting Authentication Service
- Basic authentication with Pega Platform credentials
- OpenID Connect (OIDC) for enterprise SSO integration
- Configure authentication service in the mobile Channel security settings

### Time-outs and Native Locks
- Configure auto-lock timeout to lock the app after inactivity
- Enable native device locks (PIN, pattern, biometric) as additional security layer
- Session timeout forces re-authentication after extended inactivity

### Making Mobile Apps Available to Users
- In App Studio → Users → Personas, assign a mobile Channel to the user role
- Only users with the assigned role can access the mobile app

### Configuring Pega Client for Windows OAuth 2.0
- Configure OAuth 2.0 authentication for the Windows desktop client
- Supports PKCE (Proof Key for Code Exchange) for enhanced security

## Distributing Mobile Apps

### Downloading Installation Packages
- Download generated .ipa (iOS) and .apk/.aab (Android) files from the build results
- Distribute packages for internal testing before public release

### Publishing to App Stores
- **Google Play Store**: Upload signed .aab (Android App Bundle); must target Android 15 (API 35) with PMC 13.0.0+
- **Apple App Store**: Upload signed .ipa through App Store Connect; requires App Store distribution provisioning profile

### Enterprise Mobile Management (EMM)
- Distribute apps through enterprise solutions like Microsoft Intune, VMware Workspace ONE, or MobileIron
- Supports managed app configuration and remote management

## Offline Features (Traditional UI Deep Dive)

### Flow Processing in Offline Mode
- Offline-supported flow processing features allow case advancement without connectivity
- Local actions and assignments are queued and synchronized when online

### Actions in Offline Mode
- Local Actions execute immediately on the device
- Supported actions include creating cases, advancing assignments, updating data
- Unsupported actions are hidden in offline mode

### Controls in Offline Mode
- Supported controls: text input, dropdown, radio buttons, checkboxes, date picker, autocomplete, attachment
- Attach content support after the work object is resolved
- Packaging a signature after the work object is resolved

### When Rules in Offline Mode
- Evaluate conditions locally on the device
- Support property comparisons, date comparisons, and boolean evaluations

### Decision Tables and Trees in Offline Mode
- Evaluate decision logic locally without server connection
- Decision tables support multiple condition columns and result columns
- Decision trees support nested conditions with branching logic

### Validate Rules in Offline Mode
- Client-side validation runs locally on the device
- Offline Validation API available for custom validation logic

### Function Rules in Offline Mode
- Execute function rules locally for calculations and transformations

### Data Transforms in Offline Mode
- Transform data locally using supported operations
- Offline Data Transform API for custom transformations

### Data Synchronization (Traditional UI)
- **Full synchronization**: Sends all queued Actions to server and synchronizes all Data Pages and work objects
- **Delta synchronization**: Only synchronizes changes since last sync; more efficient for large datasets
- Action queue stores local user actions while offline; pushed to server when connectivity restores
- Customizable synchronization indicators show sync status to users
- Configurable sync timeouts for synchronization operations

### Enabling Online-Only Features in Offline Mode
- JavaScript API for running activities in offline mode
- JavaScript API for scheduling activities in offline mode
- Selectively enable features that require connectivity with graceful fallback

## Adjusting Performance of Offline Apps

### Large Data Pages
- Best practices for configuring large Data Pages for offline use
- Source large Data Pages from report definitions, connectors, activities, or Data Transforms
- Create data types and Data Pages optimized for offline packaging
- Define JavaScript functions for custom data population
- Declare Data Pages as large to optimize packaging and synchronization
- Asynchronous population of large Data Pages for better performance

### Caching Data in Offline Apps
- Configure caching strategies to minimize data transfer
- Limit offline packaging to data from the user's worklist
- Disable processing of attachments and signatures in offline mode when not needed
- Configure external temporary storage options for attachments
- Configure action queue management for efficient synchronization

## Extending Mobile Apps with JavaScript

### JavaScript APIs for Offline Apps
- **Data storage APIs**: Read and write local data on the device
- **Data synchronization APIs**: Control sync behavior programmatically
- **Offline worklist APIs**: Manage offline worklist operations
- Add JavaScript code to Pega Platform applications or offline-enabled apps

## Troubleshooting Mobile Apps

### Common Issues
- **Misconfiguration and authentication issues**: Verify server URL, credentials, authentication service settings, and certificate configuration
- **Secure connection issues**: Check certificate pinning, SSL/TLS configuration, and network proxy settings
- **iOS-specific issues**: Provisioning profile mismatches, signing certificate problems, build errors
- **Case Status conflicts in offline mode**: Configure conflict resolution strategies; select Work Queues for cases with status conflicts

### Accessing Mobile Device Logs
- Access logs from the mobile device for debugging
- View mobile device logs in Pega Platform for server-side analysis
- Use logging features for containerized deployments on Kubernetes

### Best Practices for Updating Mobile Apps
- Always use the latest Pega Mobile Client for security and compatibility
- Test updates thoroughly before distributing to users
- Update mobile Channels that reuse web portals carefully
- For offline-enabled apps: plan update strategy to handle data synchronization during update

## Pega Client for Windows

### Overview
- Desktop client application for Windows that brings Pega Platform applications to the Windows desktop
- Provides a native Windows experience with Pega Platform functionality

### Building a Pega Client for Windows Application
- Configure and build the Windows client from the Pega Platform
- Supports modern Windows versions

### Supported Features
- Case management, worklist, assignments, data entry
- Generating PDF files from eForms for offline-enabled applications
- OAuth 2.0 authentication support

### What's New in Pega Client for Windows
- Latest improvements and feature additions for the Windows desktop client

## Localization
- Localize mobile apps for international audiences
- Support multiple languages through localization packs
- Upload localization files through the mobile Channel advanced branding settings

## Interview and Certification Q&A

**Q: What are the two UI architectures available for mobile apps in Pega Platform '25?**
A: Constellation (recommended) and Traditional UI (Theme Cosmos). There is also a legacy UI Kit, but it should not be used for new apps. Constellation offers low-code configuration with native components, while Traditional UI supports the full range of mobile features including full offline mode.

**Q: What offline capabilities does the Constellation architecture support?**
A: In Pega Platform '25, Constellation supports read-only offline capabilities including viewing offline-enabled Cases and Assignments, editing Case details, viewing attachments (images, PDFs), and automatic data synchronization when connectivity is restored. It does NOT support full offline mode, signature control, barcode scanning, or custom modules.

**Q: How do you set up push notifications for a mobile app?**
A: For Android, configure Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM) with the server key in Pega Platform. For iOS, configure Apple Push Notification Service (APNs) certificates or keys. Then set up notification categories and templates in the mobile Channel settings. Use the push console to send and manage notifications.

**Q: What are the supported mobile operating systems?**
A: iOS/iPadOS 17 and 18, and Android 9.0 and later. For Google Play Store submission, apps must target Android 15 (API level 35) using Pega Mobile Client 13.0.0+. Supported browsers include Safari, Chrome, and Edge Chromium (Android).

**Q: How does data synchronization work in offline-enabled Traditional UI apps?**
A: Two types: (1) Full synchronization — sends all queued actions to server and synchronizes all Data Pages and work objects. (2) Delta synchronization — only syncs changes since last sync. An Action queue stores local user actions while offline; these are pushed to the server when connectivity restores. Synchronization indicators show sync status to users.

**Q: What are the steps to generate a native mobile app?**
A: 1) Complete mobile app development (layout, content, branding). 2) Configure mobile build server settings. 3) Prepare signing certificates (Android keystore or iOS provisioning profile). 4) Trigger the build from the mobile Channel. 5) Download the installation package (.ipa for iOS, .apk/.aab for Android). 6) Test and distribute.

**Q: How do you secure a Pega mobile app?**
A: Configure authentication (Pega credentials or OIDC for SSO), enable session timeouts, set up auto-lock with native device locks (PIN, biometric), enable certificate pinning, ensure HTTPS for all communication, and assign mobile Channels to specific user roles through Personas.

**Q: What is the difference between adaptive and responsive mobile interfaces?**
A: Adaptive interfaces change how UI components display based on the user's device type (e.g., hide complex charts on mobile). Responsive interfaces automatically adjust layout using breakpoints so content reflows and resizes based on screen size without hiding elements. Both can be configured in Pega Platform.

**Q: How do you distribute a Pega mobile app to end users?**
A: Three methods: (1) Publish to Google Play Store (Android .aab) or Apple App Store (iOS .ipa through App Store Connect). (2) Enterprise Mobile Management solutions like Microsoft Intune or VMware Workspace ONE. (3) Direct distribution of installation packages for internal testing.

**Q: What features are available for offline-enabled Traditional UI apps that are not available in Constellation offline?**
A: Traditional UI offline mode supports full offline case processing, creating cases offline, advancing assignments, data collection via questionnaires, barcode scanning, signature capture, attachment upload, custom modules, decision tables/trees evaluation, validate rules, function rules, data transforms, and full read/write data synchronization.
